rule_or_instrument_area: Research Domain and Analytical Purpose assignment plus scratch-coder diagnostics
question: How should recurring pilot boundary differences be clarified without scoring coders or training them toward the production model?
general_clarification: Use the substantive research object rather than dataset provenance alone; require independent support for every additional Domain or Purpose; do not add Descriptive Monitoring automatically beside a more specific operation; apply Unclear independently by dimension; distinguish residential from general transport mobility; retain justified multi-domain readings without treating fewer labels as inherently preferable.
case_specific_guidance_prohibited: yes; case-specific guidance remains prohibited during formal coding and the calibration uses only permanently excluded pilot records
protocol_or_instrument_change: Clarification of existing rules plus candidate-0.4 through 0.6 diagnostic-instrument changes; no taxonomy or production-model change
status: Circulated; coder feedback resolved; coder-pack illustration and live REDCap QA pending.
shared_rules: Substantive object not dataset provenance; independent support for added labels; prefer the most specific visible operation; dimension-specific Unclear; residential versus transport mobility; justified multi-domain assignment
risk_considered: Post-pilot clarification could inflate later human-model agreement if disputed cases were systematically resolved in favour of the production model.
check_performed: The four calibration readings were compared as complete label sets with archived Fable 5 production classifications and the temporary pre-collapse GPT-5.5 comparison classifications.
result: Direction was mixed: 2019/015 matched both models; 2024/248 matched Fable only; 2021/056 matched GPT-5.5 only; 2021/038 used a narrower Analytical Purpose reading than either model.
conclusion: The shared clarifications did not systematically favour the production model and were derived from written taxonomy rules and visible register evidence. This qualitative bias check reduces but does not eliminate the possibility that calibration affects later human-model agreement.
feedback_requested: wording; branching; required notes; conditional fields; technical usability
operational_clarification_requested: Clearer operational guidance for sc_taxonomy_fit when register evidence is insufficient
operational_clarification_resolution: Candidate 0.6 point-of-use field note; screenshot-based illustration planned for coder start pack
covering_email_governance: The covering email stated that the pilot was not scored; pilot responses did not need revision; disagreement remained expected and informative; formal coding should remain independent
remaining_action: Add coder-pack screenshot guidance; complete fresh live REDCap runtime QA; freeze formal instrument only after remaining gates pass
